nodes.title: Agency Rallies Behind AHA’s Go Red for Women Movement
STEM Goes Red Launches with Inaugural Conference in San Antonio!
AHA’s Red Sofa Tour Brings Conversation About Heart Health Out to the Community

pageContext.content:
We’re proud to share the news that Fran Yanity, The PM Group’s President and the 2018 chair for the American Heart Association’s Go Red for Women movement, hosted her first Executive Leadership Team meeting today.  She and this dynamic team are already hitting the ground running in securing partners for the annual luncheon in February 2018 and we look forward to sharing the results of their hard work in the coming months.
 
Did you know?
 
Because of the American Heart Association and its Go Red for Women movement, more than 627,000 women have been saved from heart disease, and 285 fewer women are dying each day.
 
Women’s health guidelines, created by the American Heart Association, have educated millions of healthcare providers to recognize and treat heart disease in women.
 
Possibly the most important statistic is that the number of women aware that heart disease is the no. 1 killer and stroke is the no. 5 killer of women and men in the U.S. has jumped from 22 percent to well over 50 percent.
 
To learn more about how you can support the Go Red for Women movement, contact Fran Yanity at fran.yanity@thepmgrp.com. For information about women and heart health, visit www.goredforwomen.org.
 
About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. Over the past 30 years, the agency has continued to grow and is currently listed as San Antonio’s Largest Advertising Agency by the San Antonio Business Journal. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profit, real estate and fitness. In addition, the agency hosts an annual Charity Gala every February that to date has raised over $2.5 million for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years!  It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.
